---
title: "Development"
---

## Local setup

```bash
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest -q
```

Build and check package artifacts:

```bash
python -m build --sdist --wheel
python -m twine check dist/*
```

## Documentation

The documentation source is a Quarto website in `docs/`.

```bash
quarto preview docs
quarto render docs
```

Rendered files are written to `docs/_site/` and are not committed.

Quarto is a good fit for this project because it can combine Markdown,
scientific notation, Python examples, and API-oriented narrative in one
documentation site.

## Release checklist

1. Update code and tests.
2. Run `pytest -q`.
3. Update the version in `pyproject.toml` and `pudo_sequences/__init__.py`.
4. Rebuild with `python -m build --sdist --wheel`.
5. Check artifacts with `python -m twine check dist/*`.
6. Publish a GitHub release so the release workflow can publish to PyPI.

PyPI releases are immutable, so every public upload needs a new version.
